$comment: PolyRig v0.1 implementation plan-as-state. States: planned | in_progress | blocked | implemented | verified | rejected. Rules: update after every implementation attempt; never set 'verified' unless verification passed or manual verification is documented in notes.

features.title: Repository skeleton and bilingual README
acceptance_criteria: Directory layout matches SPEC.md section 2 (skill/, packs/, schemas/, scripts/, docs/)
README.md (en) and README.zh-CN.md exist; both state positioning, non-goals, and the cold-start demo narrative
LICENSE chosen and added
git repository initialized with first commit

manual: Tree matches SPEC layout; READMEs read coherently in both languages

features.title: Protocol schemas: pack, feature_list, manifest
acceptance_criteria: schemas/pack.schema.json covers id, type(stack|domain), version, last_reviewed, summary, requires, conflicts, provides, stacks, trust{level, scripts_enabled_by_default, requires_confirmation}
schemas/feature_list.schema.json enforces the 6-state machine and required per-feature fields (acceptance_criteria, verification, pack_refs, depends_on)
schemas/manifest.schema.json covers polyrig_version, generated_at, language, selected_packs[{id, version, source, last_reviewed, copied_to, checksum}], overrides
docs/pack-protocol.md documents the protocol in prose with examples

manual: docs/pack-protocol.md examples validate against the schemas by inspection

features.notes: Schemas are the protocol; write them before any pack content.

features.title: Zero-dependency tooling scripts
acceptance_criteria: scripts/validate-pack.mjs validates a pack directory against pack.schema.json plus structural rules (knowledge/overview.md and verify.md present; per-stack/ files match pack.yaml stacks; domain packs' requires resolvable)

scripts/build-pack-index.mjs scans the discovery roots (builtin, ~/.polyrig/packs, legacy ~/.claude/polyrig-packs, <target>/.polyrig/packs), applies project>user>builtin override precedence, and emits an index JSON including trust source and script presence per pack
scripts/link-skill.mjs installs PolyRig skills into supported agent platforms (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini CLI, OpenCode), idempotently
scripts/doctor.mjs checks node version, skill link status, and runs validate-pack over all builtin packs
All scripts run on bare node (no package.json dependencies) and exit non-zero on failure

manual: Create a deliberately broken pack; validate-pack.mjs reports the specific violation

features.notes: Deterministic work only; all user interaction stays in the skill.

features.title: Pack: stack/android
acceptance_criteria: knowledge/overview.md: project structure conventions, Gradle/AGP/Kotlin version relationships as decision guidance (no pinned versions in prose), module layering, build variants
knowledge/pitfalls.md: known traps and security red lines (e.g. keystore handling, minSdk pitfalls)
deps.yaml: coordinates + lookup strategy + official sources, version_policy: verify_latest_before_use
verify.md: build/lint/test/connected-check verification routes
passes scripts/validate-pack.mjs

manual: Author self-review: every statement is slow-changing or delegated to deps.yaml lookup

features.title: Pack: stack/backend-fastapi
acceptance_criteria: knowledge/overview.md: project layout, async patterns, settings/env management, dependency injection, API versioning as decision guidance
knowledge/pitfalls.md: known traps and security red lines (e.g. CORS, secrets in env, sync-in-async)
deps.yaml: coordinates + lookup strategy + official sources
verify.md: pytest/type-check/run verification routes
passes scripts/validate-pack.mjs

manual: Author self-review for slow-changing-only prose

features.title: Pack: domain/auth-core
acceptance_criteria: knowledge/overview.md: OAuth2/OIDC flow decision tree (which flow for which client type), token exchange, refresh strategy, server-side session vs stateless, secure storage principles per platform class
knowledge/pitfalls.md: CSRF/nonce/state handling, redirect URI validation, token leakage red lines
verify.md: auth-flow verification checklist (happy path, failure states, token persistence, revocation)
provides: [auth-core]; no provider-specific content leaks in
passes scripts/validate-pack.mjs

manual: Cross-check: nothing Google/Apple/WeChat-specific appears in this pack

features.notes: Foundation pack; F007 depends on it via requires.

features.title: Pack: domain/auth-google
acceptance_criteria: pack.yaml requires: [domain/auth-core]; stacks: [android, backend-fastapi]
knowledge/per-stack/android.md: Credential Manager / Google Identity integration decisions, ID-token flow to backend
knowledge/per-stack/backend-fastapi.md: ID-token verification, audience checks, session issuance
deps.yaml: package candidates + lookup strategy + official Google sources only
verify.md extends auth-core checklist with Google-specific checks (audience, token expiry, revoked account)
passes scripts/validate-pack.mjs including requires resolution

manual: Author self-review against current official Google Identity docs

features.title: Artifact templates
acceptance_criteria: templates/ contains SPEC.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, feature_list.json, manifest.json, init.plan.md, init.sh, deps.resolved.md
AGENTS.md template is routing + hard rules only (state-update rule, verified-gate rule); no knowledge prose
CLAUDE.md template is a thin pointer to AGENTS.md with zero duplicated content
init.sh template has set -euo pipefail, review-first header, and only creates context dirs/files (no dep installs, no build-file edits, no remote fetches)
feature_list.json and manifest.json templates validate against schemas/

manual: Read-through: templates contain placeholders, not example knowledge

features.title: Agent-platform skill: /polyrig (SKILL.md)
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md implements the seven-phase interview (identity, stack, domains, constraints, first feature, verification route, generate); 1-3 questions per phase, each with a recommended answer and default
SKILL.md is flow/routing/decision-tree/artifact-format only; all knowledge stays in packs (thin-skill rule)
Pack discovery via scripts/build-pack-index.mjs; override announcements and trust rules enforced (project-level packs: scripts never run by default; user-level: confirm first)
Assembly performs online version verification per deps.yaml lookup strategies and writes deps.resolved.md with date/source/confidence; staleness warning when last_reviewed exceeds 180 days
Generation copies selected pack knowledge into docs/stacks|domains/, writes all artifacts from templates, and records the audit chain in .polyrig/manifest.json with checksums
Language rule stated explicitly: interaction follows the user's language; generated artifacts are English
node scripts/link-skill.mjs installs it for supported agent platforms; /polyrig appears or is discoverable through the platform's managed context in a fresh agent session

manual: Dry-run the interview in a scratch directory; inspect every generated artifact against SPEC section 5

features.notes: The runtime-facing component is now platform-adapted rather than Claude-specific. Implemented and installed via link-skill smoke tests; not 'verified' because the manual dry-run of the full seven-phase interview is pending — it doubles as the F010 demo.

features.title: Golden-path cold-start acceptance demo
acceptance_criteria: A real small project (Android app + FastAPI backend, Google Sign-In) is initialized via /polyrig
All generated artifacts validate against schemas/; .polyrig/manifest.json records all 4 packs with checksums
A FRESH session with zero verbal context implements the first feature relying only on on-disk artifacts
The first feature passes its verification route (automated commands pass; manual checks documented)
feature_list.json in the demo project shows the feature transitioned planned -> in_progress -> implemented -> verified by the agent itself
The demo run is written up as the README core narrative

manual: Full cold-start run as described; reviewer confirms no verbal context was supplied to the fresh session

features.notes: This is the v0.1 release gate. If this passes, PolyRig holds. BLOCKED on interactive execution the orchestrator cannot perform alone: running /polyrig in a live session, Google Cloud OAuth client setup, a device/emulator, and a fresh zero-context session. Runbook prepared at docs/examples/golden-path-demo.md.

features.title: Authoring guide for pack authors
acceptance_criteria: docs/authoring-packs.md walks through creating a user-level pack (e.g. a voice-IME domain pack) by hand against the protocol
Documents the three discovery roots, override precedence, and trust implications for pack authors
Uses auth-google as the worked reference example
Documents Evidence Matrix, [Evidence: E001] markers, and deps.yaml evidence requirements

manual: A reader can produce a validate-pack-passing pack following only this doc
automated: node scripts/validate-pack.mjs <pack created during doc review>

features.notes: Manual authoring guide updated for Evidence Matrix. Verified historically with a hand-built voice-ime pack; new Evidence Matrix protocol is tracked by F012.

features.title: Pack authoring skill and Evidence Matrix protocol
acceptance_criteria: Evidence Matrix is a formal pack validation requirement: references/sources.md exists, ids are unique E### values, legal statuses/volatility are enforced, Markdown [Evidence: E001] references resolve, strong rules require source-backed or user-provided evidence, and deps.yaml entries require evidence arrays
All four builtin packs are minimally migrated with references/sources.md, evidence markers, and deps.yaml evidence where applicable
The /polyrig skill copies pack sources.md into generated docs/stacks|domains and includes copied sources in checksum guidance

A new skill/polyrig-pack-author exists with six-phase guided authoring, create/update behavior, web-search/source discipline, automated draft/ready gate, independent subagent validation, fixed dual subagent review prompts, and pack templates
scripts/link-skill.mjs and doctor support installing/checking both polyrig and polyrig-pack-author
README, SPEC, pack protocol, architecture, and authoring guide document Evidence Matrix and pack-authoring behavior

manual: Protocol / structure subagent review reports no blocking issues
Content / safety subagent review reports no blocking issues

features.notes: Implementation used subagents for validator, skill, /polyrig copy contract, installation, and each builtin pack migration. The skill now makes validation plus the dual review gate an automated independent phase when subagent or fresh-session contexts are available; final dual review is still required before marking verified.

features.title: Pack: stack/ios
acceptance_criteria: pack.yaml declares id: stack/ios, type: stack, provides: [ios-app], and builtin trust posture
knowledge/overview.md covers Xcode project shape, SwiftUI/UIKit decisions, state ownership, Swift concurrency, lifecycle, dependencies, privacy, signing, and distribution as stable guidance

knowledge/pitfalls.md captures iOS-specific red lines and failure modes for signing secrets, privacy manifests, sensitive storage, SwiftUI identity, MainActor/callbacks, release builds, physical-device checks, and App Review
deps.yaml routes volatile Xcode, Swift, SwiftUI/UIKit, XCTest, SwiftPM, privacy manifest, signing, and distribution lookups to official sources
verify.md defines build, test, UI test, static/project checks, simulator/device smoke checks, privacy/storage review, and release-style validation routes
references/sources.md contains an Evidence Matrix and every strong rule / deps entry cites evidence
passes scripts/validate-pack.mjs

manual: Author self-review against official Apple, Swift, and OWASP sources
Protocol / structure reviewer approved with no blocking issues
Content / safety reviewer approved with non-blocking issues and no blockers

features.notes: Ready gate completed: validate-pack passed; protocol reviewer approved; content/safety reviewer approved with non-blocking evidence-attribution suggestions and no blockers.

features.title: Pack lint: verify.md tool coverage against deps.yaml
acceptance_criteria: validate-pack.mjs flags any tool invoked in verify.md through a package runner (uv run, uvx, poetry run, pipx run, pdm run, npx, pnpm dlx/exec, yarn dlx, bunx) that has no matching deps.yaml coordinate or package_candidates entry
System toolchain commands (xcodebuild, ./gradlew, adb) are not flagged
Each missing tool is reported once per verify.md with the line of first use
packs/stack/backend-fastapi declares ruff and mypy in deps.yaml so its verify routes resolve at assembly time
All builtin packs pass validate-pack.mjs with the lint active

manual: Review that the lint messages point at the assembly-time consequence (missing deps.resolved.md entry), not just the missing declaration

features.notes: Root cause found by the TinyHabit Sync cold-start evaluation: docs/verify.md demanded ruff/mypy while deps.resolved.md never resolved them, because the pack's verify.md and deps.yaml disagreed. 2026-07-04: verified — test-validate-pack-verify-tools and test-validate-pack-evidence pass; lint caught ruff/mypy in backend-fastapi before the deps.yaml fix and all five builtin packs pass after it.

features.title: git init in the generated init flow
acceptance_criteria: templates/init.sh runs a guarded git init when the target is not already inside a git work tree, and seeds a minimal .gitignore (.env) if absent
init.sh still never installs packages, edits build files, fetches remotely, writes business code, or commits
templates/init.plan.md lists the git repo and .gitignore under created items and carries the first commit as a manual follow-up
SKILL.md's safe-operations contract and SPEC.md's init.sh row both include the guarded git init

manual: Instantiate the template in a scratch directory outside any repo, run it, and confirm git init runs; run it again inside a repo and confirm git init is skipped and no commit is made

features.notes: Root cause found by the TinyHabit Sync cold-start evaluation: the generated project was never a git repository, contradicting the core promise that the context layer enters git and travels with the repo. 2026-07-04: verified — bash -n passes; manual route executed in a scratch dir: outside any repo the instantiated script created a repo on branch main with .gitignore=.env and zero commits; nested inside an existing repo git init was skipped and the parent .gitignore was untouched.

features.title: Artifact validation tooling: validate-artifacts.mjs
acceptance_criteria: scripts/validate-artifacts.mjs validates a target project's feature_list.json and .polyrig/manifest.json against schemas/ and exits non-zero on any violation
The shared subset validator resolves local #/$defs $refs and checks date / date-time formats so the feature and manifest schemas are fully applied
SKILL.md's post-generate self-check runs the script instead of asking the agent to re-read schemas by hand
The script runs on bare node with no dependencies

manual: Run the script against a PolyRig-generated project and confirm both artifacts PASS

features.notes: Closes the v0.1 secondary gate 'generated artifacts validate against schemas/', which previously had no tooling behind it. 2026-07-04: verified — test-validate-artifacts passes; manual route executed against the TinyHabit Sync test project: both artifacts PASS.

features.title: Pack groups: versioned reference manifest, validation, discovery, install, consume, author
acceptance_criteria: schemas/group.schema.json defines groups/<name>/group.yaml (id group/<name>, version, last_reviewed, summary, members[{id,version}] non-empty, optional requires[{id,version}] for out-of-group packs); a group is the install and publish unit while member packs stay atomic

scripts/validate-group.mjs enforces the six invariants (member requires closure, member existence/version match, unique/valid ids, members-vs-requires disjoint, no member-to-member conflicts, acyclic requires graph) and PASSes groups/auth/group.yaml
scripts/build-pack-index.mjs emits a groups[] array parallel to packs (id, version, summary, last_reviewed, members, requires, source, path) with project>user>builtin override precedence

scripts/install-pack.mjs parses /groups/<name>/versions/<version>, installs the whole group in dependency-first topological order, writes a group install record at <dest>/.polyrig-groups/<name>.json plus each member's .polyrig-install.json, and soft-guides a member URL (blocks non-zero forcing --group or --single; --single pulls the requires closure but not group siblings)

schemas/manifest.schema.json adds selected_groups[] ({id, version, lock:[{id,version}]}); validate-artifacts covers it and /polyrig writes it when a group suite is selected while members still record in selected_packs[]

skill/polyrig P3 presents compatible group suites alongside single domain packs and resolves group members in dependency-first order; skill/polyrig-pack-author adds a group.yaml authoring subflow (auto-read member pack.yaml versions into the lock, run validate-group) plus a dependency pre-scan that warns 疑似无效引入，请确认 without blocking; skill/polyrig-pack-install documents group URL, soft-guide, group install record, and group update
package.json test chain includes node scripts/test-validate-group.mjs; docs/pack-protocol.md and both READMEs document the group mechanism

manual: Read-through: SKILL group flow and authoring contract match the spec; group.yaml examples use block YAML (no flow maps)
Protocol / structure and content / safety reviews report no blocking issues

features.notes: Protocol-side pack group feature (tasks 1-9). Scripts core (tasks 1-5), manifest schema (task 6), and groups/auth migration are green; tasks 7-9 add the /polyrig consume flow (selected_groups), polyrig-pack-author group authoring + dependency pre-scan, polyrig-pack-install group interaction contract, npm test wiring, and user docs. Registry-side joint upload/atomic publish is out of scope here and lands in polyrig-registry per the spec.
